serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 350 ml (350 g), equivalent to a regular-sized cup of iced fruit tea as typically served at a Malaysian café or coffee bar.

nutrient_source.title: Fruit-flavoured tea drink, ready-to-drink — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy / Foundation Foods; supplemented with IMR Malaysia MyFCD generic sweetened iced tea values
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service; Institute for Medical Research Malaysia
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for 'Sunshine Ice Fruit Tea' exists in any approved primary database. Values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central entries for ready-to-drink fruit-flavoured iced tea (NDB 14355 / FDC ID 171890) and cross-referenced with IMR Malaysia MyFCD generic sweetened cold tea values. The beverage is assumed to contain brewed tea, fruit juice or fruit pieces, sugar syrup, and ice, yielding a lightly sweetened iced tea profile. Per-100g values reflect a diluted, lightly sweetened iced tea with fruit flavouring. Estimation basis documented in notes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021 — Atkinson FS et al.; supplemented with University of Sydney GI Database entry for sweetened iced tea / fruit tea
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for 'Sunshine Ice Fruit Tea'. GI estimated at 47 based on the University of Sydney GI Database analogous entry for sweetened fruit tea / iced tea beverages (GI range 40–55 for lightly sweetened tea drinks). Atkinson et al. (2021) report GI values of 47 for sweetened iced tea. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (47 × 26.25) / 100 ≈ 12.3, where carbohydrate per 350 g serving = 7.5 × 3.5 = 26.25 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (~5 mg per 100 g; ~17.5 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Sunshine Ice Fruit Tea is very low in sodium (~17.5 mg per 350 ml serving) and poses minimal concern for hypertension; however, individuals should be mindful of added sugar content, as high sugar intake can indirectly elevate blood pressure over time.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Added sugar content (~7.0 g sugar per 100 g; ~24.5 g per serving) and moderate glycemic load (~12.3 per serving)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume Sunshine Ice Fruit Tea with caution due to its added sugar content and moderate glycemic load of approximately 12.3 per standard 350 ml serving; opting for a reduced-sugar or unsweetened version is advisable to limit postprandial glucose excursions.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Negligible fat and saturated fat content; low sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Sunshine Ice Fruit Tea contains negligible fat (0 g per 100 g) and very low sodium, making it a low cardiovascular risk beverage in terms of lipid and sodium load; however, regular consumption of the sweetened version may contribute to excess caloric intake and should be moderated as part of a heart-healthy diet.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Sunshine Ice Fruit Tea is a chilled Malaysian-style beverage consisting of brewed tea (typically black or green tea) combined with mixed fruit elements such as fresh fruit slices, fruit juice, or fruit syrup, served over ice with added sugar syrup or honey. It is a popular café and coffee-bar drink in Malaysia and Singapore, commonly offered as a refreshing cold beverage option. The drink has a light, fruity, mildly sweet flavour with a slightly tannic tea base and a refreshing iced texture. Nutritionally, it is a lightly sweetened beverage providing approximately 30 kcal per 100 ml, with the primary macronutrient being carbohydrate from added sugars (~7.5 g per 100 g). Fat content is negligible and protein content is minimal. The glycemic index is estimated at 47 (low GI), but the glycemic load per standard 350 ml serving is approximately 12.3, which is moderate, making portion control relevant for individuals managing blood glucose. Sodium content is very low (~5 mg per 100 g), making it suitable for low-sodium dietary patterns. The beverage is naturally vegan, halal, gluten-free, and dairy-free. Clinically, the main consideration is added sugar content, which is relevant for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes or those managing caloric intake. The tea polyphenol content may offer minor antioxidant benefits. This drink is typically consumed as a standalone refreshment, afternoon beverage, or accompaniment to light meals in Malaysian and Singaporean café settings.

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry for 'Sunshine Ice Fruit Tea' exists in any approved primary database. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central ready-to-drink fruit-flavoured iced tea entries and cross-referenced with IMR Malaysia MyFCD generic sweetened cold tea values. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database analogous entry for sweetened iced tea. Confidence is medium due to the analogous estimation approach rather than an exact match.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central (FDC ID 171890, ready-to-drink fruit-flavoured iced tea) and IMR Malaysia MyFCD generic sweetened iced tea values; no exact database entry exists for this specific branded menu item. 2. fat_g is 0.0: confirmed zero — iced fruit tea beverages contain no fat by formulation; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central analogous entry. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, and cholesterol_mg are all 0.0: naturally zero as this is a tea-based fruit beverage with no lipid-containing ingredients. 3. protein_g is 0.1 (trace): trace amount from brewed tea; effectively negligible. 4. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, and selenium_mcg are all -1: these micronutrients are not reported in the USDA FoodData Central analogous entry for ready-to-drink fruit-flavoured iced tea and are not available from the IMR Malaysia MyFCD source for this beverage category. 5. glycemic_index set to 47 (analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database for sweetened iced tea); gi_category is low (GI ≤ 55). 6. Serving size of 350 g is an estimate for a standard café cup; actual serving size may vary by restaurant. 7. The food is served at EC COFFEE BAR - Carpenter Street, Singapore, sourced from field survey data. 8. Price: SGD 11.80 (single restaurant, single price point). 9. Multilingual names for Chinese, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi are provided as this is a Malaysian dish served in Singapore, satisfying the Singaporean/Malaysian multilingual requirement.
